key_components: Zod schema definitions for multiple configuration domains
validateEnv() function with detailed error reporting
Helper functions for environment checks and time parsing
complexity_indicators: 12 separate schema definitions (App, Database, Redis, JWT, Security, SMTP, Storage, Logging, RateLimit, CORS, Features, External)
Complex validation with coercion and regex patterns

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple domain-specific Zod schemas composed into a single EnvSchema using spread operator
patterns_discovered.benefit: Modular, maintainable configuration structure with clear separation of concerns

patterns_discovered.description: Using z.infer<typeof EnvSchema> to automatically derive TypeScript types from validation schemas
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single source of truth for both validation and type definitions

patterns_discovered.description: Environment validation occurs at module load time (line 208: export const env = validateEnv())
patterns_discovered.benefit: Catches configuration errors immediately during startup rather than at runtime

patterns_discovered.description: Detailed error messages with field-level validation errors and user guidance
patterns_discovered.benefit: Developers quickly understand what configuration is missing or invalid

patterns_discovered.description: Using Zod's coerce and transform methods for type conversion and data restructuring
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexible environment variable handling (strings converted to numbers, arrays, etc.)

patterns_discovered.description: Utility functions abstracting common patterns (isDevelopment, parseExpiryToMs, etc.)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces repeated logic and provides single points of configuration usage

gotchas_discovered.description: JWT expiry times stored as strings (e.g., '15m', '1h') with regex validation rather than parsed upfront
gotchas_discovered.impact: Parsing happens at runtime in parseExpiryToMs(); could be done during validation
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider pre-parsing during schema validation for earlier error detection

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Optional SMTP configuration creates incomplete validation
gotchas_discovered.description: SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS are all optional without cross-field validation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could create invalid states (e.g., SMTP_HOST set but SMTP_USER missing)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add refine() validation to ensure SMTP fields are all-or-nothing

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Error formatting assumes specific error structure
gotchas_discovered.description: Error extraction assumes _errors array structure which may not match all Zod error types
gotchas_discovered.impact: Some validation errors might be silently dropped or incorrectly formatted
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use Zod's built-in .flatten() method for more robust error handling

gotchas_discovered.description: DATABASE_URL lacks a default value and will fail in development without .env setup
gotchas_discovered.impact: Development setup requires explicit database configuration
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider providing test/dev defaults or better documentation

gotchas_discovered.description: String-to-array transformation could fail silently if string is empty
gotchas_discovered.impact: Empty ALLOWED_FILE_TYPES becomes empty array instead of validation error
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation to ensure array is non-empty after transformation

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive Zod-validated environment configuration module
key_achievements: Implemented 12 domain-specific schemas covering all critical application configuration areas
Established type-safe environment variable handling with full TypeScript inference
Created fail-fast validation at application startup
Provided helpful error messages for configuration issues
Added utility functions for common configuration queries
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - Well-documented, modular, and follows validation best practices
approach_outcome.test_coverage_readiness: Good foundation for unit testing of validation logic

recommendations.recommendation: Add cross-field validation for dependent configuration values
recommendations.details: Use Zod's .refine() method to ensure SMTP configuration is consistent (either all present or all absent), and validate JWT secret strength requirements are met

recommendations.recommendation: Replace manual error formatting with Zod's .flatten() or .pretty() methods
recommendations.details: Lines 177-185 manually extract errors which may miss nested validation failures. Use built-in methods for robustness

recommendations.recommendation: Create a .env.example file documenting all environment variables
recommendations.details: Extract schema information from this module to auto-generate .env.example with descriptions and defaults

recommendations.recommendation: Pre-parse time duration strings during validation
recommendations.details: Instead of storing JWT expiry as strings and parsing later, validate and store as milliseconds upfront using Zod's transform
Ensure the split array is non-empty after transformation on line 75-77
recommendations.impact: Prevents silent failures with empty file type lists

recommendations.recommendation: Add JSDoc examples to EnvSchema and helper functions
recommendations.details: Include examples of reading specific config values and using helper functions

recommendations.recommendation: Create validation test file testing all schema paths
recommendations.details: Unit tests for validateEnv(), parseExpiryToMs(), and all environment checks
